channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Lessons from the Iran war
item.description: The past month and a half have shown that the nature of modern warfare is shifting.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire talks: What are the key sticking points?
item.description: Talks between Iran and the US expose deep divisions, prolonging uncertainty during a two-week ceasefire.

item.title: Who is running US foreign policy? Varsha Gandikota and Jeremy Scahill
item.description: Varsha Gandikota-Nellutla asks journalist Jeremy Scahill who will benefit from the US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: What happened in 40 days of war on Iran
item.description: Who got hit in the war on Iran? The attacks, the damage and the people caught in between.

item.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 44 of the US-Iran conflict?
item.description: US-Iran talks in Islamabad end without a deal, with each side blaming the other for the failure.

item.title: Pakistan urges US and Iran to uphold ceasefire after talks end
item.description: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has insisted the US and Iran must uphold their ceasefire agreement.

item.title: US and Iran fail to reach a deal after marathon talks in Pakistan
item.description: US VP Vance says Iran is choosing not to accept US terms, while Iran says it did not expect a deal at the first meeting.

item.title: Oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Three supertankers laden with oil exit waterway amid global energy crunch, shipping data shows.

item.title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is 'what happens next?'
item.description: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

item.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
item.description: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.
